news.headline: Novo Nordisk's denecimig (Mim8) shows long-term safety and efficacy in Phase 3 FRONTIER4 hemophilia A study at ISTH 2026
news.summary: Interim FRONTIER4 data presented at the ISTH Congress in Paris showed the investigational bispecific antibody cut annualized bleeding rates by up to 99% versus on-demand treatment across adults, adolescents and children with hemophilia A. Denecimig is under FDA review after a BLA filed in September 2025.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk withdraws a CagriSema device-comparison study
news.summary: Novo Nordisk withdrew a trial designed to compare two injection-device versions of CagriSema in people with obesity, a study that had only been submitted in January 2026. The move refocuses investor attention on CagriSema's core obesity data ahead of its expected FDA decision later in 2026.

news.headline: Medicare's new GLP-1 Bridge program begins, covering Wegovy at a $50 monthly copay
news.summary: Starting July 1, eligible Medicare beneficiaries with obesity can access Wegovy (injection and pill) for a flat $50 monthly copay through the CMS-run Bridge demonstration, running through the end of 2027. The program significantly expands government-funded access to Novo Nordisk's flagship weight-loss drug in the US.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk discloses major cyberattack after refusing a $25 million extortion demand
news.summary: Novo Nordisk confirmed attackers, including a group calling itself FulcrumSec, exfiltrated non-public data — including deidentified clinical-trial information, source code and internal AI models — after gaining access via a leaked GitHub token. The company refused the ransom demand and said core business operations were unaffected, though some healthcare providers were notified.

news.headline: Wegovy oral pill prescriptions surpass 3 million as Novo Nordisk unveils new data at ADA 2026
news.summary: Just over five months after its US launch, the Wegovy 25mg tablet passed 3 million prescriptions — roughly one every five seconds — with over 80% going to patients new to GLP-1 therapy. The milestone coincided with the commercial rollout of Wegovy HD, a higher 7.2mg injectable dose, and new post-hoc data from SELECT, STEP, ESSENCE and OASIS presented at the ADA Scientific Sessions.

news.headline: Wegovy pill launches in the UAE, Novo Nordisk's first market outside the US
news.summary: Following approval by the Emirates Drug Establishment, Novo Nordisk launched the oral Wegovy tablet in the United Arab Emirates for weight management and cardiovascular risk reduction, marking its first commercial rollout outside the US. The company has flagged further international Wegovy-pill launches for the second half of 2026.

catalysts.label: H1/Q2 2026 financial results
catalysts.detail: Novo Nordisk reports interim results for the first half of 2026, including any update to full-year sales and operating-profit guidance.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on CagriSema for weight management
catalysts.detail: FDA review of the CagriSema (cagrilintide/semaglutide) NDA, filed December 2025 on REDEFINE 1/2 data; company guidance points to a decision around year-end 2026, though CagriSema missed its noninferiority endpoint versus tirzepatide in a head-to-head study earlier in 2026.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on denecimig (Mim8) for hemophilia A
catalysts.detail: Biologics license application submitted September 2025; standard review timing points to a decision in the second half of 2026, following strong long-term FRONTIER4 safety/efficacy data shown at ISTH 2026.

catalysts.label: Further international launches of the oral Wegovy pill
catalysts.detail: Building on the June 2026 UAE debut, Novo Nordisk plans to roll out the Wegovy tablet in additional select markets outside the US during the second half of 2026.

catalysts.label: Phase 1 readout for next-generation subcutaneous GLP-1 analogue
catalysts.detail: A single-ascending-dose study in roughly 48 people with overweight/obesity, part of Novo's post-CagriSema/amycretin obesity pipeline, is expected to read out in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.label: New CagriSema study to support a revised dosing/device strategy
catalysts.detail: After withdrawing a device-comparison trial and testing new dose-escalation regimens for next-gen obesity assets, Novo Nordisk is designing a new CagriSema study aimed at a 2026 regulatory submission.

pulse: Novo Nordisk is defending its GLP-1 franchise against intensifying Eli Lilly competition while leaning on the blockbuster oral Wegovy pill (over 3 million US prescriptions, first ex-US launch in the UAE) and new access wins like the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge's $50 copay starting July 2026. The company is simultaneously managing a major cyber-extortion incident disclosed in June and a mixed next-gen pipeline picture: CagriSema missed a head-to-head noninferiority endpoint versus tirzepatide but still awaits an FDA obesity decision expected around Q4 2026, while denecimig posted strong long-term hemophilia A data at ISTH 2026 ahead of its own FDA filing decision. Under new CEO Mike Doustdar, Novo is pursuing bolt-on M&A (Akero) and signaling ambitions beyond weight loss into longevity and aesthetics as it works to rebuild growth momentum after 2025's guidance cuts and workforce restructuring.
